title: Zillow Group Reports First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results
text: Zillow Q1 2026 Financial Results and Rental Business Growth. Q1 2026 Key Metrics: Revenue increased 18% year-over-year to $708 million, with strong contributions from all segments. Residential revenue rose to $445 million, up 9% year-over-year. Mortgages revenue of $64 million represented a 56% year-over-year increase, driven by a 96% increase in purchase loan origination volume to $1.5 billion. Rentals revenue increased 42% year over year to $183 million, primarily driven by multifamily revenue growing 57% year over year. Profitability: The company achieved a net income of $21 million, representing the first quarterly GAAP profit on a trailing-twelve-month basis. Adjusted EBITDA was $170 million, representing a 24% margin. Earnings Per Share: EPS came in at $0.53, beating analyst estimates of $0.45. Business Growth Highlights: Multifamily property count grew to approximately 75,000 properties, continuing its strong momentum in the rental segment. The company expanded its AI mode feature availability and continued integrating AI tools across its platform to improve user engagement. Market Conditions: Despite operating in a relatively flat housing market, Zillow achieved strong revenue growth by leveraging product innovations, particularly AI-driven features and expanded rental market penetration.
